text: Hello friends, myself Dr. Renu Singh from Kanpur going to present a video emphasizing the role of laparoscopic conservative dissection with minimal adduceolysis followed by post-op diagnosis for three to two months which facilitates spontaneous conception rates. This patient is a twenty-eight years old female patient with primary infertility and ultrasound revealing bilateral ovarian endometriomas.

During laparoscopy, the surgeon observes a normal-sized uterus and bilateral ovarian endometriomas, with complete obliteration of the pouch of Douglas. Diluted vasopressin is injected into the uterus and ovarian endometriomas using a dilution of ten units in one hundred milliliters of normal saline.

The surgeon facilitates the avascular plane of dissection during ovarian cystectomy, using contraction and counter traction techniques to conserve the underlying ovarian follicles visible within the ovarian tissue.

The surgeon performs conservative dissection using blunt and sharp techniques with gold scissors, avoiding energy sources. Dense adhesions in the pouch of Douglas are cleared, revealing both ovarian endometriomas.

The surgeon performs an ovarian cystectomy on the left side using traction and counter traction techniques, avoiding the use of energy sources to minimize damage to the serosa and mesothelial cells, thereby reducing the risk of adhesion recurrence.

The surgeon clears the pouch of Douglas (POD) of thick, dense adhesions using traction, counter traction, cold scissor dissection, and blunt dissection. The ovarian cyst on the right side is drained, and cystic knee is performed with traction and counter traction.

The surgeon drains the cyst to reveal the cyst wall, emphasizing the importance of avoiding unnecessary dissection, particularly urethrolysis and retroperitoneal dissection, to minimize the risk of re-adhesions.

Saline irrigation is continuously applied to keep the tissue hydrated and protect the peritoneal mesothelial cells. After completing two cystectomies on the right side, the surgeon reveals a third cyst and performs cystectomy in the appropriate plane of dissection.

The whole concept to be emphasized upon in infertile patient with grade III-IV endometriosis should be to restore the uterine mobility, to clear bout of Douglas with adhesions. To do ovarian cystectomy without using any energy source so as to conserve the maximum ovarian follicles pooled in the ovary and to do the fimbrioplasty bilaterally.
The surgeon releases significant adhesions of the left sigmoid colon from the lateral pelvic wall. Subsequently, 500 ml of saline is instilled into the cavity.
